text: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting with business succession. [Sector forecast](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/sectorprognoses/index.html). Healthcare entrepreneurs appear to be less concerned with business succession than entrepreneurs in other sectors. This is worrying because more and more healthcare entrepreneurs are approaching retirement age.

Healthcare entrepreneurs appear to be less concerned with business succession than entrepreneurs in other sectors. metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: Healthcare entrepreneurs appear to be less concerned with business succession than entrepreneurs in other sectors. This is worrying because more and more healthcare entrepreneurs are approaching retirement age. Business succession is important for continuity for patients, sufficient supply and the pension income of healthcare entrepreneurs. In addition to non-profit foundations, the healthcare sector also has many entrepreneurs. The vast majority of healthcare entrepreneurs are self-employed or sole proprietorships.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: This is worrying because more and more healthcare entrepreneurs are approaching retirement age. Business succession is important for continuity for patients, sufficient supply and the pension income of healthcare entrepreneurs. In addition to non-profit foundations, the healthcare sector also has many entrepreneurs. The vast majority of healthcare entrepreneurs are self-employed or sole proprietorships. More than 50,000 of the almost 270,000 entrepreneurs have multiple employees.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: Business succession is important for continuity for patients, sufficient supply and the pension income of healthcare entrepreneurs. In addition to non-profit foundations, the healthcare sector also has many entrepreneurs. The vast majority of healthcare entrepreneurs are self-employed or sole proprietorships. More than 50,000 of the almost 270,000 entrepreneurs have multiple employees. Of the healthcare entrepreneurs with employees, more than 37,000 have a company in curative care, for example in oral care, physiotherapy, pharmacy or general practitioner care.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: In addition to non-profit foundations, the healthcare sector also has many entrepreneurs. The vast majority of healthcare entrepreneurs are self-employed or sole proprietorships. More than 50,000 of the almost 270,000 entrepreneurs have multiple employees. Of the healthcare entrepreneurs with employees, more than 37,000 have a company in curative care, for example in oral care, physiotherapy, pharmacy or general practitioner care. Outside of curative care, care entrepreneurs are mainly active in residential care, for example with a care farm or other housing initiative, or in home care.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: The vast majority of healthcare entrepreneurs are self-employed or sole proprietorships. More than 50,000 of the almost 270,000 entrepreneurs have multiple employees. Of the healthcare entrepreneurs with employees, more than 37,000 have a company in curative care, for example in oral care, physiotherapy, pharmacy or general practitioner care. Outside of curative care, care entrepreneurs are mainly active in residential care, for example with a care farm or other housing initiative, or in home care. Nearly 6,000 healthcare entrepreneurs with employees are now 65 years or older, a significant increase from the 2,000 in 2010.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: More than 50,000 of the almost 270,000 entrepreneurs have multiple employees. Of the healthcare entrepreneurs with employees, more than 37,000 have a company in curative care, for example in oral care, physiotherapy, pharmacy or general practitioner care. Outside of curative care, care entrepreneurs are mainly active in residential care, for example with a care farm or other housing initiative, or in home care. Nearly 6,000 healthcare entrepreneurs with employees are now 65 years or older, a significant increase from the 2,000 in 2010. The fact that healthcare entrepreneurs are getting older means that they have to think about succession for their company and security for their patients.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: Of the healthcare entrepreneurs with employees, more than 37,000 have a company in curative care, for example in oral care, physiotherapy, pharmacy or general practitioner care. Outside of curative care, care entrepreneurs are mainly active in residential care, for example with a care farm or other housing initiative, or in home care. Nearly 6,000 healthcare entrepreneurs with employees are now 65 years or older, a significant increase from the 2,000 in 2010. The fact that healthcare entrepreneurs are getting older means that they have to think about succession for their company and security for their patients. Ipsos I&O conducted a survey on business succession among 534 owners of companies with employees on behalf of ABN AMRO.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: Outside of curative care, care entrepreneurs are mainly active in residential care, for example with a care farm or other housing initiative, or in home care. Nearly 6,000 healthcare entrepreneurs with employees are now 65 years or older, a significant increase from the 2,000 in 2010. The fact that healthcare entrepreneurs are getting older means that they have to think about succession for their company and security for their patients. Ipsos I&O conducted a survey on business succession among 534 owners of companies with employees on behalf of ABN AMRO. This shows that more than half of the 38 healthcare entrepreneurs surveyed are currently not concerned with business succession.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: Nearly 6,000 healthcare entrepreneurs with employees are now 65 years or older, a significant increase from the 2,000 in 2010. The fact that healthcare entrepreneurs are getting older means that they have to think about succession for their company and security for their patients. Ipsos I&O conducted a survey on business succession among 534 owners of companies with employees on behalf of ABN AMRO. This shows that more than half of the 38 healthcare entrepreneurs surveyed are currently not concerned with business succession. In other sectors, a lot fewer entrepreneurs, about a third, are not yet involved in business succession.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: Growth expectations make healthcare companies a popular purchase. Healthcare entrepreneurs seem to have their hands full with providing sufficient good care. The healthcare sector is growing rapidly. This year by 3 percent and next year by as much as 3.5 percent, ABN AMRO expects. Due to an aging population and increasing prosperity, the demand for care will also continue to increase in the longer term. metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: In addition to higher healthcare volumes, rates that healthcare providers receive in almost all sectors are rising more than inflation, improving profit margins. Only in elderly care are rates rising slightly less rapidly than inflation. The growth expectation makes healthcare companies a popular purchase for healthcare providers, other entrepreneurs and investors. Dental practices, residential care initiatives and pharmacies are particularly popular. Figures from the Dutch Healthcare Authority (NZa) show that the number of approved mergers and acquisitions of organizations with more than fifty healthcare providers employed increased to 214 last year.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: Only in elderly care are rates rising slightly less rapidly than inflation. The growth expectation makes healthcare companies a popular purchase for healthcare providers, other entrepreneurs and investors. Dental practices, residential care initiatives and pharmacies are particularly popular. Figures from the Dutch Healthcare Authority (NZa) show that the number of approved mergers and acquisitions of organizations with more than fifty healthcare providers employed increased to 214 last year. The chains and larger healthcare companies mainly acquired companies and practices in oral care (62 approved applications), long-term care (41 approved applications) and pharmaceutical care (32 approved applications).

text: The chains and larger healthcare companies mainly acquired companies and practices in oral care (62 approved applications), long-term care (41 approved applications) and pharmaceutical care (32 approved applications). Divide workload with multiple owners. The succession issue has not yet been resolved with a business transfer. When the entrepreneur retires, someone else must be found to manage the company. metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: Divide workload with multiple owners. The succession issue has not yet been resolved with a business transfer. When the entrepreneur retires, someone else must be found to manage the company. In addition to running the company, that successor must also provide care. For example, novice GPs usually take over a general practice from GPs who retire.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: The succession issue has not yet been resolved with a business transfer. When the entrepreneur retires, someone else must be found to manage the company. In addition to running the company, that successor must also provide care. For example, novice GPs usually take over a general practice from GPs who retire. In certain healthcare segments and regions, for example in regions with an older population, successors are difficult to find.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: When the entrepreneur retires, someone else must be found to manage the company. In addition to running the company, that successor must also provide care. For example, novice GPs usually take over a general practice from GPs who retire. In certain healthcare segments and regions, for example in regions with an older population, successors are difficult to find. The interest in entrepreneurship among young healthcare providers seems to be decreasing, [the Capaciteitsorgaan states](https://capacityorgaan.nl/app/uploads/2023/01/Capaciteitsplan-2024-2027-Deelverslag-2-Huisartsgenee-DEF-12-januari.pdf).

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: In addition to running the company, that successor must also provide care. For example, novice GPs usually take over a general practice from GPs who retire. In certain healthcare segments and regions, for example in regions with an older population, successors are difficult to find. The interest in entrepreneurship among young healthcare providers seems to be decreasing, [the Capaciteitsorgaan states](https://capacityorgaan.nl/app/uploads/2023/01/Capaciteitsplan-2024-2027-Deelverslag-2-Huisartsgenee-DEF-12-januari.pdf). General practitioners have difficulty finding successors for their practice because the 'new generation' of general practitioners values ​​part-time working, which is difficult to combine with an individual general practice.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: For example, novice GPs usually take over a general practice from GPs who retire. In certain healthcare segments and regions, for example in regions with an older population, successors are difficult to find. The interest in entrepreneurship among young healthcare providers seems to be decreasing, [the Capaciteitsorgaan states](https://capacityorgaan.nl/app/uploads/2023/01/Capaciteitsplan-2024-2027-Deelverslag-2-Huisartsgenee-DEF-12-januari.pdf). General practitioners have difficulty finding successors for their practice because the 'new generation' of general practitioners values ​​part-time working, which is difficult to combine with an individual general practice. As a result, the number of practices with one GP in general practitioner care is decreasing.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: In certain healthcare segments and regions, for example in regions with an older population, successors are difficult to find. The interest in entrepreneurship among young healthcare providers seems to be decreasing, [the Capaciteitsorgaan states](https://capacityorgaan.nl/app/uploads/2023/01/Capaciteitsplan-2024-2027-Deelverslag-2-Huisartsgenee-DEF-12-januari.pdf). General practitioners have difficulty finding successors for their practice because the 'new generation' of general practitioners values ​​part-time working, which is difficult to combine with an individual general practice. As a result, the number of practices with one GP in general practitioner care is decreasing. The number of group practices with multiple general practitioners as owners has actually increased, from 35 percent in 2020 to 43 percent in 2024, according to figures from knowledge organization [Het Nivel](https://www.nivel.nl/sites/default/files/files/1004735_0.pdf).

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: The interest in entrepreneurship among young healthcare providers seems to be decreasing, [the Capaciteitsorgaan states](https://capacityorgaan.nl/app/uploads/2023/01/Capaciteitsplan-2024-2027-Deelverslag-2-Huisartsgenee-DEF-12-januari.pdf). General practitioners have difficulty finding successors for their practice because the 'new generation' of general practitioners values ​​part-time working, which is difficult to combine with an individual general practice. As a result, the number of practices with one GP in general practitioner care is decreasing. The number of group practices with multiple general practitioners as owners has actually increased, from 35 percent in 2020 to 43 percent in 2024, according to figures from knowledge organization [Het Nivel](https://www.nivel.nl/sites/default/files/files/1004735_0.pdf). Young general practitioners more often opt for shared ownership or employment with a larger general practitioner organization or at a health center.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: General practitioners have difficulty finding successors for their practice because the 'new generation' of general practitioners values ​​part-time working, which is difficult to combine with an individual general practice. As a result, the number of practices with one GP in general practitioner care is decreasing. The number of group practices with multiple general practitioners as owners has actually increased, from 35 percent in 2020 to 43 percent in 2024, according to figures from knowledge organization [Het Nivel](https://www.nivel.nl/sites/default/files/files/1004735_0.pdf). Young general practitioners more often opt for shared ownership or employment with a larger general practitioner organization or at a health center. This way they can divide the workload and responsibilities and realize economies of scale.

Young general practitioners more often opt for shared ownership or employment with a larger general practitioner organization or at a health center. This way they can divide the workload and responsibilities and realize economies of scale. Lack of follow-up threatens healthcare provision and pensions.

This way they can divide the workload and responsibilities and realize economies of scale. Lack of follow-up threatens healthcare provision and pensions. The lack of suitable successors has consequences for the continuity of care. metadata.source_title: Healthcare entrepreneurs are waiting for business succession

text: The lack of suitable successors has consequences for the continuity of care. For example, patients must look for another GP, pharmacy, physiotherapist or dentist if a practice cannot find a successor. The consequences are greater for clients who live with a ceasing care provider because they have to move to another care farm or care home. Healthcare entrepreneurs attach relatively great importance to the revenue from their company in order to provide for their pension. More than a third of the healthcare entrepreneurs interviewed by Ipsos I&O see the financial return as one of the most important aspects of the succession and transfer of their company, while for entrepreneurs in other sectors this is on average only a fifth.

More than a third of the healthcare entrepreneurs interviewed by Ipsos I&O see the financial return as one of the most important aspects of the succession and transfer of their company, while for entrepreneurs in other sectors this is on average only a fifth. Read more in the healthcare sector. In the coming years, the sector will change to provide more care with fewer resources. The demand for care continues to increase due to an aging population, while it is hardly possible to attract more healthcare personnel. Innovation and collaboration make work easier and more fun, ensuring that healthcare provision remains sustainable and of high quality.
